title: Quick check: Exclusions and audience hygiene

questions.prompt: What's the minimum exclusion every prospecting campaign should have?

choices.text: No exclusions — let the algorithm decide.

Past purchasers (60-180 days depending on repeat cycle) and current customers via CRM upload — so prospecting budget doesn't repaint people you already have. For Advantage+ Shopping, use the existing-customer budget cap (20-30%) instead of explicit exclusion. Don't over-exclude: excluding all site visitors 90d kills retargeting; excluding lookalikes from broad is counterproductive.

questions.explanation: Exclude existing customers from prospecting. Don't over-exclude beyond that.

questions.prompt: Retargeting frequency hit 14, CPA climbing. Fix?

choices.text: Three levers: (1) expand the retargeting window (7d → 14d or 14d → 30d) so more users enter the pool and frequency averages down, (2) add more retargeting creatives so rotation distributes impressions across messages, (3) rotate to a different angle (e.g., social-proof message after the cart-abandon message). Frequency >10 with rising CPA = ad fatigue; refresh the audience pool or the creative variety.

questions.explanation: Fatigue. Expand window, add creatives, rotate angle.
